If the probability of task completion is lower than the previous probability and the robot is not already changing its plan, then the plan should be changed. If the mission is not complete and a problem is detected or if the robot is not following the current path, the robot should change its plan.

explanation: This LTL formula addresses the scenario where time constraints are present. If time constraints are present (timeConstraints), then the plan should change (changePlan). If time constraints are present, then the probability of completing the task should be assessed to check whether it's a problem, or if the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete). The robot should then explain why, in each case. It should also provide a justification for why it is changing the plan if time constraints are present (provideReason), and explain the path that the robot will take. The robot also has to give an explanation and justify its decisions.

if time constraints are present, the robot should explain why, in each case
The robot has to give an explanation and justify its decisions

output_LTL: G(timeConstraints -> (changePlan & ( (probabilityTaskCompletion_lessThan_previousProbabilityTaskCompletion | taskCannotComplete) -> explain) & provideReason & explainIntendedPath & (explain & justifyDecision)))
